title: rapistrum rugosum subsp. microcarpum
intro: Rapistrum rugosum subsp. microcarpum, also known as small-fruited rapistrum, is a subspecies of the Rapistrum rugosum plant, a type of flowering plant in the mustard family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The seeds of Rapistrum rugosum subsp. microcarpum have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and used as a medicinal tea or added to tinctures and infusions to leverage their medicinal properties.
